mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in declining rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration that reduces household income expectations and consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, forcing corporate earnings downgrades and producing negative equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint decline yields positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2.0 percent month-over-month signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing strength, driving short-term positive market momentum and 5-day equity gains.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during high inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: CPIAUCSL surpassing 230.0 indicates entrenched inflationary pressure that forces anticipated monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of threshold crossings over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls directly correlates with expanding corporate revenue bases and wage growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as labor market strength supports economic expansion.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window despite consistent payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales, where retail sales decline while GDP remains positive, signals weakening consumer momentum that precedes negative equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Divergence pattern yields positive returns in more than 55 percent of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contraction coinciding with rising unemployment triggers a recessionary supply-demand mismatch, causing immediate negative market sentiment and 5-day drawdowns in equity indices.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns during liquidity injection episodes with central bank intervention.
